sections.paragraph:
μεταφυτεύω 1 aor. pass. impv. μεταφυτεύθητι (cp. φυτόν; Theophr., HP 2, 6, 3; 4; En; Ps 91:14 Aq., Sym., Theod.; Ps 1:3 Aq.) transplant pass. be transplanted Lk 17:6 D.—DELG s.v. φύομαι C.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

impv. impv. = imperative

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 642
